title: Telegram Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Telegram** exposes the **Bot API** at `https://api.telegram.org`. Unlike most REST providers, **the bot token is part of the URL path itself** (`/bot{bot_token}/{method}`) and every response is wrapped in a uniform envelope: `{"ok": bool, "result": ..., "description": str?, "error_code": int?, "parameters": {...}}`.

This connector — `TelegramConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "telegram"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from a Telegram bot:

| Surface | Telegram method group | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Updates | `getUpdates`, `setWebhook`, `deleteWebhook`, `getWebhookInfo` | Inbound delivery — long polling **or** webhook |
| Messages | `sendMessage`, `sendPhoto`, `sendDocument`, `editMessageText`, `deleteMessage`, `forwardMessage` | Outbound text + media |
| Chats | `getChat`, `getChatMember`, `getChatAdministrators` | Chat + membership inspection |
| Files | `getFile` | Resolve `file_id` → downloadable path |
| Callback queries | `answerCallbackQuery` | Inline-keyboard button ack |
| Webhooks | provider-pushed | Telegram POSTs Update JSON to the URL set by `setWebhook`, with the install-field `webhook_secret_token` echoed in `X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token` |

The connector normalises inbound `Message` objects (from `getUpdates` or webhook) into `NormalizedDocument` (`id = f"{connector_id}_{chat_id}_{message_id}"`, tenant-scoped through `connector_id`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429 with the `retry_after` hint Telegram always supplies and 5xx/network with exponential backoff, and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::TelegramHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Create a bot with @BotFather

1. Open Telegram and message **@BotFather**.
2. Send `/newbot` and follow the prompts (name + username).
3. BotFather returns the **bot token** — format `123456:ABC-DEF1234...`. Treat it like a password.
4. (Optional) Send `/setdomain`, `/setjoingroups`, `/setprivacy` to harden the bot for your use case.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Telegram**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `bot_token` (secret) | Token from BotFather |
| `webhook_url` (text, optional) | HTTPS URL Telegram will POST updates to. When set, `install()` calls `setWebhook`. |
| `webhook_secret_token` (secret, optional) | 1–256 char secret echoed in `X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token` |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://api.telegram.org` (rarely needed — self-hosted Bot API server) |
| `default_parse_mode` (text) | `HTML` (default) / `MarkdownV2` / `Markdown` |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Client-side soft cap, default 1800 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates the token is present, calls `getMe` to confirm it works, and (if `webhook_url` is set) registers the webhook via `setWebhook`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which probes `getMe` as a lightweight liveness check. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `OFFLINE + EXPIRED`. On 403 → `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with structured-log warning that includes the Telegram-supplied `retry_after` seconds.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Telegram Bot API uses **bot-token-in-URL** authentication (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). There is no `Authorization` header, no OAuth dance, no token refresh, no expiry — the token rotates only when you `revoke` via BotFather.

### Credential model

- `bot_token` is **part of the URL path**: every request is built as `f"{base_url}/bot{bot_token}/{api_method}"`. The HTTP client owns this convention; the connector layer never touches it.
- File downloads use a different prefix: `f"{base_url}/file/bot{bot_token}/{file_path}"`. `http_client.file_url(token, file_path)` builds it for you.
- Webhook authentication is **separate** from the bot token: when `webhook_secret_token` is set, Telegram echoes it back in `X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token` on every webhook POST so the receiver can authenticate the source (constant-time compared inside `process_callback`).

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `bot_token`. Calls `getMe`. If `webhook_url` set, calls `setWebhook`. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a `TokenInfo` whose `access_token` is the bot_token (no exchange happens). |
| `health_check()` | `GET /getMe`. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — no token lifecycle. |
| token storage | None — token lives in `self.config`. |

### URL contract

Every outbound request:

```
{base_url}/bot{bot_token}/{api_method}
e.g. https://api.telegram.org/bot123456:ABC.../sendMessage
```

No `Authorization` header. No `Content-Type` header is required by Telegram for GET; for POST httpx adds `application/json` automatically because we pass `json=...`.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `bot_token` | secret | yes | `self.bot_token` | URL path segment from `@BotFather` |
| `webhook_url` | text | no | `self.webhook_url` | HTTPS URL Telegram POSTs updates to |
| `webhook_secret_token` | secret | no | `self.webhook_secret_token` | Verified in `process_callback` via constant-time compare against `X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token` header |
| `base_url` | text | no | `self.base_url` (defaults `https://api.telegram.org`) | Sandbox / self-hosted Bot API override |
| `default_parse_mode` | text | no | `self.default_parse_mode` (default `HTML`) | Default for outgoing messages when caller omits override |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 1800) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["bot_token"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_TELEGRAM_BASE = "https://api.telegram.org"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `TelegramConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP + envelope unwrap live in `client/http_client.py::TelegramHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validates `bot_token`, calls `getMe`, optionally calls `setWebhook` if `webhook_url` is configured.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth — returns a `TokenInfo` wrapping the bot_token for SDK ABI compatibility.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
`GET /getMe`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Drains pending updates from `getUpdates` (short poll, `timeout=0`) into the KB. Uses `last_update_id` metadata checkpoint; `full=True` resets it.

children.title: Bot Identity
children.content:
### `async get_me() -> Dict`
`GET /getMe`. Returns `{id, is_bot, first_name, username, can_join_groups, can_read_all_group_messages, supports_inline_queries}`.

children.title: Messages + Media
children.content:
### `async send_message(chat_id, text, parse_mode=None, disable_web_page_preview=False, reply_to_message_id=None, reply_markup=None) -> Dict`
`POST /sendMessage`. `parse_mode` defaults to `self.default_parse_mode`. `reply_markup` accepts inline keyboards (`{inline_keyboard: [[{text, callback_data}, ...]]}`).

### `async edit_message(chat_id, message_id, text, parse_mode=None) -> Dict`
`POST /editMessageText`.

### `async delete_message(chat_id, message_id) -> bool`
`POST /deleteMessage`.

### `async send_photo(chat_id, photo_url, caption='', parse_mode=None) -> Dict`
`POST /sendPhoto`. Passes the URL string as the `photo` field — Telegram fetches it.

### `async send_document(chat_id, document_url, caption='', parse_mode=None) -> Dict`
`POST /sendDocument`.

### `async forward_message(chat_id, from_chat_id, message_id) -> Dict`
`POST /forwardMessage`.

children.title: Updates + Webhooks
children.content:
### `async get_updates(offset=None, limit=100, timeout=0) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /getUpdates`. Long polling when `timeout>0`. Returns the raw list of Update objects.

### `async set_webhook(url, secret_token=None, allowed_updates=None) -> bool`
`POST /setWebhook`. Pass `secret_token` to have Telegram echo it back in `X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token` on every POST.

### `async delete_webhook(drop_pending_updates=False) -> bool`
`POST /deleteWebhook` — bot returns to short-polling mode.

### `async get_webhook_info() -> Dict`
`GET /getWebhookInfo`. Returns current URL, last error, pending count, allowed_updates filter.

### Webhook routing (override-friendly)

- `async handle_webhook(payload, headers)` — verifies the secret-token header via `process_callback`, picks the populated update subfield, dispatches via `handle_event`.
- `async process_callback(payload, headers)` — constant-time compares the `X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token` header against the configured `webhook_secret_token`.
- `async handle_event(event)` — dispatches `event["type"]` to `_handle_<kind>` (override per kind).
- `async batch_processor(items)` — drain a queue of update dicts, swallow per-item errors.

children.title: Chats + Members + Files
children.content:
### `async answer_callback_query(callback_query_id, text=None, show_alert=False) -> bool`
`POST /answerCallbackQuery`.

### `async get_chat(chat_id) -> Dict`
`GET /getChat`.

### `async get_chat_member(chat_id, user_id) -> Dict`
`GET /getChatMember`.

### `async get_chat_administrators(chat_id) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /getChatAdministrators` — group/channel admins.

### `async get_file(file_id) -> Dict`
`GET /getFile`. Returns `{file_id, file_unique_id, file_size, file_path}`. Build the download URL with `connector.http_client.file_url(bot_token, file_path)`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
TelegramError                          # base; status_code + error_code + response_body
├── TelegramBadRequestError            # 400
├── TelegramAuthError                  # 401
├── TelegramForbiddenError             # 403 (bot kicked / blocked)
├── TelegramNotFound                   # 404 (chat / message / method)
├── TelegramConflictError              # 409 (getUpdates + webhook concurrent)
├── TelegramRateLimitError             # 429 — carries `retry_after` from Telegram envelope
├── TelegramServerError                # 5xx
└── TelegramNetworkError               # transport (DNS / TLS / timeout)
```

Back-compat alias preserved: `TelegramNotFoundError = TelegramNotFound`.

### Retry behaviour (`helpers/utils.py::with_retry`)

| Failure | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Sleep for `retry_after` (Telegram **always** supplies this on 429); fall back to exponential backoff with jitter. Up to 3 retries by default. |
| 5xx / `TelegramNetworkError` / `httpx.HTTPError` | Exponential backoff `base_delay * 2^attempt + jitter`, capped at `MAX_RETRY_DELAY_S`. Up to 3 retries. |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches typed exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)   — logged as warning with retry_after
network → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, CONNECTED)
other → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `bot_token` blank | Re-enter in ACP. BotFather can re-issue a token if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 — bot token revoked via BotFather (`/revoke`) or wrong characters | Confirm the token matches `@BotFather → /mybots → <bot> → API Token` exactly. Re-issue if needed. |
| `403 Forbidden: bot was blocked by the user` on `send_message` | The target user blocked the bot, or the bot was kicked from a group | Only retriable after the user unblocks / re-adds the bot. Don't keep retrying — caps your `send_message` budget. |
| `409 Conflict: terminated by setWebhook` on `get_updates` | A webhook is currently registered; `getUpdates` is incompatible | Either `delete_webhook()` first, or stop polling. The connector raises `TelegramConflictError` so callers don't loop blindly. |
| `telegram.rate_limit — retrying` warnings spike | Hitting 429 — Telegram per-method / per-chat cap | The client honours `parameters.retry_after`. Throttle outgoing message rate (Telegram's documented limit ≈ 30 msg/s globally, 1 msg/s per chat for groups). |
| Webhook POST received but `result.verified=false` | `webhook_secret_token` mismatch | Confirm the secret in ACP exactly matches the value passed to `setWebhook` originally. The connector compares with `hmac.compare_digest` (constant-time). |
| `handle_webhook` returns `{status: "ignored"}` | Update kind isn't one we route (e.g. `shipping_query`) | Subclass `TelegramConnector` and add `_handle_<kind>` to opt in. The router rejects only — it does not 5xx. |
| File download URL fails | Stale `file_path` (Telegram rotates them) | Call `get_file(file_id)` again to get a fresh `file_path`, then `http_client.file_url(token, file_path)`. |
